message:
perf: reduce allocations in CBOR canonicalization, HPKE AAD, transport path selection, symbol store

Five allocation-reduction improvements across hot paths:

fcp-cbor/lib.rs:
- Pre-allocate scratch buffer in canonicalize_map() with
  Vec::with_capacity(entries.len() * 32) — typical CBOR map keys are
  10-50 bytes each, so 32 bytes/entry is a reasonable estimate that
  avoids multiple reallocations during key sorting.

fcp-crypto/canonicalize.rs:
- Add to_deterministic_cbor_with_capacity() that pre-allocates the
  output buffer when the typical serialized size is known. The existing
  to_deterministic_cbor() delegates to it with capacity=0.

fcp-crypto/hpke_seal.rs:
- Fcp2Aad::encode() now uses with_capacity(128) — typical AAD CBOR is
  ~120 bytes (32 zone + 32 node + 16 purpose + 8 timestamp + overhead).

fcp-mesh/transport.rs:
- Replace filter+collect+sort with retain+sort-in-place in
  TransportSelector::select_paths(). Eliminate intermediate group Vec
  by sorting the priority-group slice in-place and iterating references.
  Pre-allocate selected Vec to min(fanout, eligible.len()).

fcp-store/symbol_store.rs:
- Pre-allocate symbols HashMap::with_capacity(source_symbols) when
  registering a new object, avoiding rehashes as symbols arrive.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
